features.title: Client catalog and adapter manifest loading
features.summary: client list loads verified and generic client targets from a Rust-side catalog instead of hard-coding one narrow set of brands in the schema.
features.nextStep: Keep the catalog current and move install/export logic into manifest-driven patchers instead of runtime brand branches.

features.title: Surface-aware client catalog
features.summary: The catalog distinguishes local, cloud, and API connector surfaces so compatibility is modeled by surface instead of brand alone.
features.nextStep: Keep adding documented surfaces and retire assumptions that one product name implies one transport/auth model.

features.title: Client, session, and project context resolution
features.summary: client plan resolves client identity, project roots, cwd, session signals, and richer metadata without starting a hub runtime.
features.nextStep: Reuse this logic from the future hub instead of duplicating it.

features.title: MCP roots and optional _meta context parsing
features.summary: The planner understands MCP-style roots objects, file:// URIs, and optional com.mcpace/context hints.
features.nextStep: Preserve the same contract in the future stdio shim and HTTP ingress.

features.title: Derived internal session leases
features.summary: When an external session id is missing, the planner derives a stable fallback lease and warns that the future hub must keep it sticky.
features.nextStep: Carry the derived lease model forward into the live hub and ingress layers.

features.title: Project root resolution and ambiguity handling
features.summary: The planner resolves project roots from flags, env, single-root metadata, or roots+cwd and warns when multiple roots remain ambiguous.
features.nextStep: Mirror these rules in the live hub and session registry.

features.title: Credential profile aware partitioning
features.summary: Credential-scoped servers now prefer an explicit credential profile id and only fall back to coarse binding classes when necessary.
features.nextStep: Feed this with real credential profile identities once the runtime owns auth flows.

features.title: Conservative scope and concurrency arbitration
features.summary: Unknown scope classes and unknown concurrency policies fall back to isolation or conservative serialization instead of optimistic sharing.
features.nextStep: Keep the same conservative defaults when the live hub starts reusing instances.

features.title: Surface constraint warnings during planning
features.summary: client plan warns for tools-only, public-http-only, no-remote-oauth, deprecated SSE, and tool-budget surfaces.
features.nextStep: Keep the warning layer honest and promote only those constraints that become runtime-enforced behavior.

features.title: Runtime scenario fixture set
features.summary: The repo carries production-like runtime fixtures separated into typical, edge, adversarial, compatibility, and held-out cases.
features.nextStep: Keep adding real regressions from future traces and failures.

features.title: Surface-aware coverage and gap reporting
features.summary: mcpace lab now reports coverage and gaps by client family, surface class, surface kind, and documented constraints.
features.nextStep: Use this surface breakdown to prioritize cloud/local divergence work instead of only counting scenarios.

features.title: Held-out release gate lane
features.summary: Held-out runtime fixtures exist so release gates can reserve scenarios that day-to-day iteration does not tune against.
features.nextStep: Keep at least one release-only scenario blocked from daily optimization.

features.title: Live hub daemon lifecycle
features.summary: The repo now ships `init` plus `hub up/down/status/logs` with file-backed state, health, stop-signal handling, and structured event logs.
features.nextStep: Layer stdio/HTTP ingress, session/instance ownership, and lease enforcement on top of the current lifecycle shell.

features.title: stdio shim that normalizes initialize metadata
features.summary: The repo does not yet ship a stdio shim that injects cwd/_meta hints and forwards to a persistent daemon.
features.nextStep: Add mcpace stdio-shim so stdio hosts do not need a thick runtime per launch.

features.title: Local Streamable HTTP ingress with session handling
features.summary: The repo does not yet expose a live Streamable HTTP endpoint, Origin handling, or MCP session lifecycle.
features.nextStep: Add a local HTTP ingress that owns session creation, Mcp-Session-Id handling, and safe localhost-only defaults.

features.title: Exclusive lease enforcement for single-session servers
features.summary: The planner can warn about exclusive ownership, but no live runtime currently enforces or revokes those leases.
features.nextStep: Implement a lease manager with owner tracking, heartbeat and timeout, and clear takeover rules.

features.title: Cancellation and stale-result guards
features.summary: There is no runtime protection yet for transport 404/session reset, cancel-after-restart, or stale responses landing in a new lease.
features.nextStep: Track in-flight requests per lease, propagate cancellation, and drop results that belong to dead sessions.

features.title: Health supervision and restart policy
features.summary: There is no live supervisor for start failures, degraded instances, or backoff-based restarts.
features.nextStep: Add per-instance state, restart backoff, and drain/degraded transitions.

features.title: Client install and export flows
features.summary: The repo can inspect client surfaces, but cannot yet patch or export real client configs.
features.nextStep: Implement client install and export as manifest-driven config patchers instead of hard-coded brand logic in the runtime core.

features.title: Safe config merge and owned-block patching
features.summary: No code yet proves that install and export will preserve user-managed MCP entries and only mutate the MCPace-owned block.
features.nextStep: Add merge tests against real-looking client configs before shipping installers.

features.title: Real-client compatibility matrix
features.summary: The repo has a surface-aware catalog, but not yet a nightly lab against real Codex, Claude, Cursor, Kiro, Windsurf, Copilot, Gemini, or Hermes surfaces.
features.nextStep: Build a compatibility lane that captures initialize, session, roots, and auth traces from real hosts and turns them into held-out regressions.

features.title: Tools-only surface enforcement
features.summary: Cloud/API connector surfaces like Claude API MCP connector and Copilot cloud agent need stronger runtime feature gating than warnings alone.
features.nextStep: Teach install/export and the future runtime to withhold resources/prompts where the surface is documented as tools-only.

features.title: Tool-budget-aware export and enablement
features.summary: Some surfaces have documented tool-count budgets; MCPace should eventually budget exposure instead of enabling every discovered tool.
features.nextStep: Track per-surface tool budgets and build export heuristics that can keep high-value tools within those limits.

features.title: Cloud or brokered relay path
features.summary: A purely local daemon cannot cover every cloud or API-driven MCP surface that expects a public HTTP endpoint.
features.nextStep: Design a separate relay or public HTTP lane after the local correctness slice is proven.
